reasoning: The flagged 'malicious command' is a commented line in PKGBUILD that isn't executed during build/install. Qwen misclassified this static comment as executable code. Deepseek's comprehensive review found no actual code execution risks.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly interpreted an obfuscated comment as executable code, while Deepseek properly analyzed execution context.
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to review 10 critical files (start-tor-browser, execdesktop, etc.) that Deepseek analyzed
Qwen skipped verification of actual executable scripts while focusing only on 3 base files
false_positives: Qwen's PKGBUILD verdict: Commented text can't execute during makepkg (AUR packages don't 'run' comments)
learnings: Improve comment handling: Train models to distinguish between code vs. non-executable comments
Enforce minimum file coverage: Require review of all build/install hooks and PKGBUILD-referenced files
Add PKGBUILD execution phase awareness: Focus analysis on code that actually runs during build/install stages
